question: Write a C++ program that uses dynamic memory allocation to create an array of squares. Get array size from user, dynamically allocate memory, fill array with squares of indices (0², 1², 2², etc.), display the array, and properly deallocate memory.

errors.description: No input validation: Function doesn't check for negative or zero size values before allocating memory.
No null pointer check: PrintArray function doesn't verify if myArray is null before dereferencing.
No input validation: Program doesn't validate user input for size, allowing invalid values.
No memory allocation check: Program doesn't verify if 'new' operation succeeded before using the pointer.
